content.claim: Entry to 6/8-year gimnázium tracks is stratified by socioeconomic background; families must invest substantial financial, social, and cultural capital to compete for places, and elementary schools serving disadvantaged populations often do not facilitate student participation in the entry competition.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Study design and sample scope not fully specified in available summaries
Stratification finding is consistent with but does not definitively prove that selection operates as parental-resource sorting rather than pure ability identification
No quantification provided of the magnitude of financial/time investment required by families
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
